l3574: You''re going to need some fortified armaments if you wish to survive out there, Cerim. Talk to Fillmore the blacksmith - he''ll reinforce your gear. Once you''ve done some upgrades, come speak to me. OBJECTIVE: Upgrade Weapons
l3578: There’s only one way to live up to your own legend, Cerim. Show me your true mettle and cut down the enemies of Sacrament. There’s good coin in it, if glory and duty aren’t reward enough. OBJECTIVE:
l3580: An army is more than just men. It’s the food in their bellies, the weapons in their fists, the clothes on their back. Fetch me what I need, and you’ll be handsomely rewarded. OBJECTIVE:
l4434: The natural world offers man her bounty. And what do we offer her in return? Order. The wilderness''s rewards await those who tame her – rewards that I trust you''ll put to good use. OBJECTIVE: Mine Copper Ore Veins
l3582: Cerim, we''re still discovering secrets on this island to this day - You''d be doing us a favor if you could help map out the surrounding areas. Time to go on patrol. Patrol Area:
l3576: As you may know, the bandits and raiders roaming Sacra often stash their weaponry in chests scattered across the land. Large chests often have the most valuable offerings. Cerim, steal back what they stole from us. OBJECTIVE: Loot Chests
l3584: With the right materials, Eleanor can enchant your weapons and armor with mystical properties. Show me some enchanted gear, and I''ll offer you a reward. You need all the strength you can get. OBJECTIVE: Enchant Weapons
l4436: The natural world offers man her bounty. And what do we offer her in return? Order. The wilderness''s rewards await those who tame her – rewards that I trust you''ll put to good use. OBJECTIVE: Fish at Fishing Spots
l4438: It pains me to say this, Cerim, but survival might require more than traditional weapons alone. Visit Eleanor the enchanter to master runes. An occult weapon is still better than a shattered one. OBJECTIVE: Attach Runes to Weapons
l4439: The natural world offers man her bounty. And what do we offer her in return? Order. The wilderness''s rewards await those who tame her – rewards that I trust you''ll put to good use. OBJECTIVE: Chop Down Trees
l3586: Would you like to see Sacrament returned to its former glory, Cerim? The citizens of the city are hard at work bringing their home back to its prime. Seek out Danos at the market and see if you can lend a hand. OBJECTIVE: Complete Builder Projects
l3588: We all need to lend a helping hand to our fellow comrades in these trying times, Cerim. Show me that you stand with Sacrament and you shall have this reward. OBJECTIVE: Complete Challenges
l3596: I often say that Parry and Riposte are a soldier''s bread and butter. The latter fills the stomach, the former keeps it from being split open. If you want to be of any use to us, you''ll need to master this technique. OBJECTIVE: Perform Successful Parries
l4441: The natural world offers man her bounty. And what do we offer her in return? Order. The wilderness''s rewards await those who tame her – rewards that I trust you''ll put to good use. OBJECTIVE: Chop Down Birch Trees
l3593: With the right materials, Eleanor can enchant your weapons and armor with mystical properties. Show me some enchanted gear, and I''ll offer you a reward. You need all the strength you can get. OBJECTIVE: Enchant Armors
l4442: It pains me to say this, Cerim, but survival might require more than traditional weapons alone. Visit Eleanor the enchanter to master runes. An occult weapon is still better than a shattered one. OBJECTIVE: Extract Runes from Weapons
l3606: I heard that Elsa woman found an entrance to some mysterious ruins beneath the cemetery in Sacrament. If you venture through them, report back to me. OBJECTIVE: Complete Cerim Crucible Runs
l4444: The plague is a mad dog, and it''s finally snapped its leash. My men report fresh outbreaks cropping up all over the island. Scour those areas clean, Cerim, before the whole of Sacra is lost. OBJECTIVE: Cleanse Pestilence Outbreak Regions
l4446: The Balak Taw have grown rowdy yet again, raiding parties probing our borders. Perhaps if you face down their champion once more, it will put them back in their place. At least for a time. OBJECTIVE: Rematch the Unbreakable Egg in Marin Woods
l3595: I''ve heard rumblings of Roan and the old man performing some kind of twisted experiments with the blood of those sick, plague-infected creatures - Whatever he''s doing, I don''t trust that bumbling idiot with the remains of devils. Cerim, if you happen to find any of this substance, please show it to me. I''ll rest easier knowing it''s in my hands. OBJECTIVE: Collect Plague Ichor
l3600: As you may know, the bandits and raiders roaming Sacra often stash their weaponry in chests scattered across the land. Large chests often have the most valuable offerings. Cerim, steal back what they stole from us. OBJECTIVE: Loot Large Chests
l3599: You''re going to need some fortified armaments if you wish to survive out there, Cerim. Talk to Mirabelle and Meriweather, the tailor sisters - they will reinforce your gear. Once you''ve done some upgrades, come speak to me. OBJECTIVE: Upgrade Armors
l4447: The natural world offers man her bounty. And what do we offer her in return? Order. The wilderness''s rewards await those who tame her – rewards that I trust you''ll put to good use. OBJECTIVE: Unearth Digging Spots
l4449: If you manage to obtain any stray furnishings, Cerim, I would gladly take them off your hands to help the destitute of Sacrament. But please, don''t mention my charitable endeavors to others. I''ve a reputation to uphold. OBJECTIVE: Collect Furniture
l3608: Murderers, plunderers, thieves... It’s a sad fact that we are beset by those who have chosen an ill-fated path. Bring these lawbreakers to justice. OBJECTIVE: Complete Bounties
l4452: We''ve reports of strange embers scattered across Sacra, hot to the touch and said to be fragments of fallen stars. All I care is if they can make my men''s shields stronger and spears sharper. I''ll reward you handsomely for any you find. OBJECTIVE:
l3610: It seems the plague can poison the mind of man and beast alike. These rabid animals need to be put down, Cerim, or else the sickness might spread. Location:
l3612: More of those Nith tribe monstrosities have crawled out of the swamp. The plague has rotted their souls, and the bog has rotted their bodies. Drive them back beneath the muck. Location:
l3614: Locals have reported Torn Crawlers burrowing out of the earth. They drag down wild animals when we’re lucky, people when we’re not. The Crawlers have already dug their grave - you just need to finish the job. Location:
l3618: Strange rumors have been circulating about the plagued women who escaped from the prison. Supernatural hokum, I’m sure. I’ll squash these rumors, and you squash these “witches.” Location:
l3620: My scouts say they’ve spotted another Risen patrol. Criminals pretending to be revolutionaries. We must stem the tide of their so-called rebellion before it boils over into outright war. Location:
l3622: This might be an unpopular opinion, but I’ve no pity for the plagued prisoners. I reserve my sympathies for the innocent, thank you. Whatever their crime once was, the punishment now is death. Location:
l3624: Nasty little buggers, aren’t they? You strike down one Siren only for two more to wriggle free. Cut through the swarm, Cerim, before a nuisance becomes an infestation. Location:
l3616: I hear reports of one of the feared Huntsmen bringing down quarry both animal and man. There’s a fine line between sportsman and madman. Make him your trophy, Cerim. Location:
l3655: One of my Guards was infected by the Pestilence. Poor bastard tried to hide it. Maybe if he''d told us sooner we could''ve cut off the limb. Now... it all has to go. Location:
l4453: I take no pleasure in sending one of my own Guard to an early grave, but we must remind ourselves it is a mercy, not a punishment. The Pestilence already killed his soul, now you just need to make sure the body follows suit. Location:
l3629: It seems I finally learned what’s worse than an armored Risen bandit terrorizing our island. Two of them! Take down this pair of hulking brutes and you’ll earn my thanks. Location:
l3631: Grown men dressed in feathers, chirping and squawking at one another - it would be a joke if not for the sharpness of their spears. Bring these crazed Balak Taw back down to earth, Cerim. Location:
l3633: They say there’s a bright light when you die. Maybe that’s just The Gloam, pulling you down into the dirt. Track down these plagued cave dwellers and snuff out their light for good. Location:
l3637: That they used to be men is not our concern, Cerim. The plague has taken their minds, and now it falls upon us to dispatch what remains of their bodies. It’s not murder - it’s janitorial work. Location:
l3639: Legends claim The False Ones are graverobbers foolish enough to put on Cerim armor and now cursed for eternity. I’ll leave the children’s stories, and the False Ones, to you. Location:
l3641: The Harvesters once felled trees in service of Sacrament. Now it’s our duty to cut these poor souls down. If only the plague poisoned our isle’s dilettantes and socialites instead of her laborers and loggers. Location:
l3635: One of the unhinged Plague Doctors has been spotted in the wild, attacking anyone she deems “infected.” I pity whatever loss drove her to madness, but she’s beyond saving now. Location:
l3643: One of the deranged chicken knights of the so-called “House of Knellap” is on the loose. Knellap might not be real, but these knights sadly are. Dispatch him with prejudice, Cerim. Location:
l3645: I’ve received word - unlikely as it might sound - that the Knights of Knellap, the Huntsmen, and the Plague Doctors have convened a gathering in secret. I can think of only one common interest that would bring those three factions together - our ruin. Well if it’s union they seek, Cerim, unite them in death. Location:
l4456: With the Mountain Gate destroyed, it seems we''ve opened a door to evil itself. Those grotesque bat creatures have poured forth all over the island. Show no mercy - they must be eradicated if Sacra is to survive. Location:
l4458: What foul abomination is this, what sin against God? Slithering across the ground, its malformed face retching up Torn foes? But no, I don''t need to understand it. I just need you to kill it. Location:
l4460: I''ve reports of a Golden Titan rampaging through the countryside. Some say it''s a cursed graverobber, trapped within the very Cerim armor he tried to steal. But I don''t care for rumors, only results. Location:
l3649: I’ve no idea who’s footing the bill for these haughty Tanth mercenaries to be terrorizing our shores, but their gold is no match for our steel. If we can’t send them back to Tanth, let’s send them to an early grave. Location:
l3651: I take it you’ve heard stories of the Boarskin - feral, inbred pillagers claiming to be the true heirs to Sacrament? Well I wish they were only stories. Cut them down, Cerim - blood is the only language they understand. Location:
l4462: It took two of my men to drag home the remains of a poor soul who had a run-in with a Harvester giantess - one carried his top half, the other his lower. Cut her down to size before she hurts anyone else. Location:
l3653: My men say they saw… well they can’t quite seem to describe it. A scurrying mass of flesh and limbs. But it had teeth. And a terrible hunger. That’s really all you need to know. Location:
l3657: Finley''s Finest Finds is now open for bussiness.

l3789: The ship''s captain told us to try and get to the city of Sacrament. But he warned that more of the raiders who attacked our ship may lurk up ahead.
l3790: We found the eastern exit of the fortress. But the heavy gate is closed, and the lever seems to have been sabotaged.
l3791: We met a blacksmith named Fillmore. He told us that the city of Sacrament lies east of the fortress, through the Orban Glades.
l3792: We met a blacksmith named Fillmore. He told us to get to a city called Sacrament, through the Orban Glades to the east.
l3793: We encountered a mutated abomination outside Sacrament''s gate. No doubt this must be one of the victims of the Pestilence. Now we can proceed to enter the city.
l3794: We met the governor of Sacrament, a man named Ellsworth. He told us to find "The Watcher" up in the Rookery tower, near the center of town.
l3795: A young scholar named Roan instructed us to gather in the Rookery. Once we are all present, we will meet the Watcher who summoned us to the island.
l3796: We met the Watcher, an elder scholar of the Cerim. His apprentice, Roan, gave us temporary lodgings beneath the Rookery. We are now free to explore Sacrament.
l3797: Winnick told us about a warlord named Darak who fights for the Risen. He''s been spotted in the western reaches of the Orban Glades.
l3798: We tracked down Darak, but he escaped. We should report back to Winnick in Sacrament.
l3799: We overheard Winnick talking with Odessa about the encounter with Darak. Winnick seemed confident of Darak''s defeat... but he admitted it might be worth listening to what Odessa has to say.
l3800: We spoke with a captured raider named Rattigan, who claimed there is an impending attack on Sacrament. He said he could help prevent it… if we find a way to get him out of that cage.
l3801: We overheard two guards talking about their drunk colleague who was sent to wash off in a fountain. They said he has the key to the cages.
l3802: We helped Rattigan escape, and he said to meet him at the entrance to Sacrament''s sewers, down on the coast. There may yet be a chance to stop the Risen''s attack.
l3803: We''ve gained access to the sewers beneath Sacrament. We need to eliminate the raiders and find Darak before they breach the city.
l3804: The Pestilence is loose in these sewers. The Risen who were on their way to attack Sacrament are being torn apart from within. We must find Darak.
l3805: We defeated Darak, who had been taken by the Pestilence. Sacrament is no longer in danger. Winnick revealed that he knew about our deal with Rattigan, who will now be sent to the Mountain Gate as punishment.
l3806: Odessa believes there must be more to Darak''s scheme. She urged us to question some of the other captured Risen nearby.
l3807: The Watcher has a grave warning for us – we must return to the Mountain Gate and stop Madrigal Seline from breaking it down, at any cost.
l3808: Primus Bainbridge said outsiders are barred from reaching the Mountain Gate. But if we hunt down the Bolein deserters to the west, he might let us pass.
l3809: We struck down the Bolein deserters. We should return to Primus Bainbridge with proof.
l3810: We can now ascend toward the Mountain Gate. We should hurry - there may still be time to stop Madrigal Seline.
l3811: Speaker Lucian chose to punish Primus Bainbridge by casting him into a great pit of fire. We will have to get across the pit somehow in order to reach the Mountain Gate.
l3812: We arrived at the Gate too late. The Inquisition tore it open, and a horde of demons has been unleashed upon Sacra. We should venture through the mountain to see what remains of Caylen.
l3813: The holy city of Caylen has fallen. Madrigal Seline''s vanguard is in ruins. All we can do now is return to Sacrament and inform the Watcher.
l3814: We have been summoned by Madrigal Seline. We must travel through the Nameless Pass, northeast of Sacrament, to meet with her if we can.
l3815: A survivor in the quarry warned us that a faction of escaped inmates fled toward the Cerim ruins beyond the prison. We must be on our guard as we search for the Madrigal.
l3816: The Madrigal believes we can help her open the ancient gate which has sealed off the city of Caylen. She thinks the key lies in a hidden chamber nearby.
l3817: The escaped prisoners known as the Riven Twins were hiding out in the chamber. We defeated them; now we can approach the altar and attempt to open the gate.
l3818: The Sayer has pledged that the Mountain Gate will not open until the Pestilence has been defeated. It seems we have no choice but to continue exploring the island.
l3819: The Madrigal has dismissed us as a heretic, and vowed to tear down the gate herself. We are no longer welcome here. We should return to Sacrament.
l3820: Fillmore''s apprentice Will has heard that the Pestilence is encroaching upon Marin Village, where his parents live. He''s asked if there''s anything we can do to stop the plague from spreading further north.
l3821: We have entered the Marin Woods. There seem to be some kind of... voices... coming from the forest.
l3822: The plague has already taken its toll on Marin Village. We encountered Odessa, and she told us to seek the Dasha Mender Irmgard, somewhere to the west of town.
l3823: Irmgard believes the source of the evil lies within a Dasha temple in the Marin Woods. Odessa told us to seek the Winged Door, which will give access to the treetops controlled by the Balak Taw.
l3824: We''ve entered the territory of the Balak Taw. Now we must be on our guard as we make our way toward the Temple of Day and Night.
l3825: We defeated Alden, the caretaker of the temple. With any luck this will silence the insidious voices. We should return to Marin Village.
l3826: We spoke to Irmgard - she thanked us for defeating Caretaker Alden but also seems heartbroken by his death. We should return to her home in the village and look for Odessa.
l3827: It will take time for Marin to heal. But with the Caretaker gone, the village is safe again. Odessa and Irmgard, on the other hand, still have much to resolve.
l3828: We received a mysterious letter from a beggar. He asked us to deliver it to Ellsworth in the War Room.
l3829: The letter we delivered has summoned Ellsworth to a place called the Weeping Sisters, south of Sacrament in the Lowland Meadows. He suggested we might follow him and Winnick to keep watch against the plague.
l3830: We have entered the Lowland Meadows. We must keep an eye out for Ellsworth and Winnick as we head further south.
l3831: Ellsworth revealed to us the contents of the unsigned letter. But the way is blocked by putrid water flooding from somewhere nearby. Ellsworth suggested we ask the hunters at their camp to the east.
l3832: The hunters told us the water must be drained from the reservoir inside an abandoned Savor den to the west. Lara said she''ll open the way for us.
l3833: Lara unlocked the lift to an old lookout tower. She warned us of what we might find in what''s left of the lumbermill and Savor den.
l3834: We released the water from the reservoir. We should return to Ellsworth and Winnick.
l3835: The hunters'' camp was attacked by a plagued beast, and Reed''s daughter was taken. Ellsworth asked if we could pursue the creature while they tend to the injured hunters.
l3836: We dispatched Lara, who had been melded with a Torn bear. We should continue onward to meet Ellsworth and Winnick at the Weeping Sisters nearby.
l3837: The author of the letter was a man named Baurus. He warned Ellsworth that his brother King Harol was murdered, and to beware of Madrigal Seline.
l3838: We found one of the hunters wounded in the forest. Whatever did this must be lurking up ahead at their camp.
l3839: Caroline the innkeeper told us her husband has gone missing down at the Shallows, near Mariner''s Keep.
l3840: We found Gordon, Caroline''s husband, trapped on a broken bridge at the coast. We need to find another way around so we can lower the ladder for him.
l3841: We rescued Gordon from the ruins at the coast. He thanked us and said to look for him back in Sacrament.
l3842: The arena master in Sacrament told us we must have the Champion''s Crest if we wish to compete in his combat trials. But the former champion has disappeared in the Nameless Pass.
l3843: We found the Champion''s Crest in the Nameless Pass. We should bring it back to Boroth in Sacrament.
l3844: We returned the Champion''s Crest to Boroth, and he has re-opened Sacrament''s combat arena.
l3845: A young scholar named Roan advised us that if we collect any more “plague ichor” during our travels, we should bring it back to the Watcher in Sacrament’s Rookery.
l3846: With the Mountain Gate breached, the Pestilence rages worse than ever across the island. We must return to Sacrament and tell the Watcher.
l3847: Roan has asked us to venture into the plagued outbreaks and bring any Torn materials we find back to the Watcher.
l3848: The Watcher crafted some sort of effigy for us. We should make an offering at a fire pit inside of a Pestilence Outbreak, to summon the heart of the infection.
l3849: We should continue to hunt the plague, and bring Torn materials back to the Watcher as we find them. We must hold the Pestilence at bay as long as we can.
l3850: We met a historian named Elsa, who told us about a mysterious ruin beneath the stone mason''s shop near the cemetery.
l3851: We made a blood offering inside the Cerim ruin, and a doorway opened - revealing a much larger chamber below.
l3852: Elsa believes the chamber was an ancient training ground. If we make a blood offering, we can undertake these Cerim trials as our ancestors did.
l3853: Elsa discovered an inscription which tells of a band of revered warriors known as the Echo Knights. But the text is incomplete. She asked if we know of any scholars in Sacrament who might be able to help.
l3854: We spoke with Roan about the mystery of the Echo Knights. He said he will look into it... All we can do for now is keep exploring the Crucible, and wait for word from the Rookery.
l3855: Roan found an old tome with a complete translation of the inscription from the Crucible. We should take it to Elsa immediately.
l3856: Elsa has learned that Cyvion and the other Echo Knights are not heroes, but cursed wraiths who stalk these chambers, hunting any Cerim that remain. If we continue to hone our skills in the Crucible, we must be on our guard.
l3857: We felled Cyvion, the corrupted Cerim champion. But he and his Echo Knights still stalk these chambers, neither living nor dead. We must continue to hone our skills in these endless gauntlets, as our ancestors did.
l4465: We met Eleanor the enchanter. She asked us to check on a family living in a house in Beggar’s Alley to whom she sold a powerful rune.
l4466: The husband and wife were sick with the Pestilence, and took their own lives to save their child.
l4467: We should bring the rune to Eleanor.
l4468: Eleanor showed us how she can use runes to enhance the power of both our weapons and ourselves.
l3858: We met a strange alchemist named Markos. He asked us to retrieve a rare flower called Iona''s Bloom from inside a cave in the Orban Glades.
l3859: We found Iona''s Bloom, but as soon as we touched it... Something is... The words unravel. A new path reveals...   We must climb higher...…
l3860: We failed to make it back to Markos. We must attempt to reach Iona''s Bloom again.
l3861: It was impossible to retrieve Iona''s Bloom; the flower tore the phrases and transformed the cave around us. We should return to Markos.
l3862: Markos revealed that his request was a test to determine if we are really Cerim. Now he says he will return to Sacrament and make his potions available for sale there.
l4905: The Seneschal has unlocked the ability to cleanse the [!sPlagued]Pestilence[/s] from Isola Sacra.
l5018: You''ve obtained Food! Eating food typically restores [IconHealth], but some may provide temporary buffs instead.
l4907: Upon death, you are spoken anew at your last Whisper. Every death reduces your gear''s durability.
l4909:
During combat, a [!sNegative]limited[/s] amount of food may be consumed.

[!sMuted]The hunger limit lifts upon exiting combat.[/s]
l4910: Over your journey, you’ll uncover more of Isola Sacra in your map.
l4912: While [!sHeavy][!iWeight] Heavy[/s], temporarily stagger nearby enemies with a [!sHighlight][action_shoulder_barge][/s].
l4913: [run] Sprint or [dodge] Dodge and press [attack] to perform a Combo Attack. Sprinting in combat consumes [IconStamina].
l4914: Approach the wall next to the door and press [interact] to sidle along the wall.
l4916:
Raw food can be eaten as a last resort, but a [!sHighlight]cooked meal[/s] will always be stronger.

[!sHeadingMuted]Cooking[/s]
Cook meals at fire pits using gathered resources. Find recipes to unlock new meals.
l4918:
Refined materials are necessary for [!sHighlight]upgrades[/s] and [!sHighlight]crafting[/s].

Materials can be refined at upgraded vendor stalls, or at refineries that you own.
[!sSubtext]Visit the Maker in Sacrament to upgrade vendor stalls or purchase refineries.[/s]
l4919: [!sHighlight][action_sneak][/s] behind enemies until a [IconSkull] is visible, then press [attack] to [!sHighlight]backstab[/s].
l4921: Certain objects are breakable. They may reveal hidden loot or paths when destroyed.
l4923:
[!sHighlight][!tnobr][shield] Ready a Shield[/tnobr][/s] to block enemy attacks and reduce damage received.

[!sSubtext]Shields do not reduce [IconPoise] Poise Damage.[/s]
l4925:
Enemies slain within [!sPlagued]Pestilence Outbreaks[/s] may drop plagued resources. 

Extract and refine them at the Watcher, or use them to upgrade gear at a blacksmith.
l4927: Climbing surfaces like vines will consume [IconStamina]. If [IconNoStamina] runs out while climbing, you will fall.
l4929:
Burn resources to [!sHighlight]increase[/s] an outbreak''s plague level.

A higher level means more challenging corruptions, but also greater rewards.
l4931: The Crucible is an ancient training ground where Cerim can undertake various trials and challenges. Every time you enter a trial within the Crucible, you will find a different series of chambers. You must clear all the enemies from each chamber in order to advance to the next one. Dying in the Crucible will return you to the Atrium, and you must begin again from the start. If there are other Cerim still inside the trial, you must wait for them to die or complete it before you can start again.
l4933:
You can interact with fellow Cerim by pressing [interact].

Press [!sHighlight]trade[/s] to exchange or gift items. Press [!sHighlight]inspect[/s] to view another Cerim''s profile.
l4935: You can swap the selected food item.
l4937:
Fallen Embers can be used to [!sHighlight]add[/s] or [!sHighlight]re-roll[/s] enchantments on magical or plagued gear.

Cerim who have proven themselves worthy can also use them to re-assign attributes.
l4939:
[!sHighlight][action_food][/s] to restore [IconHealth].
[!sSubtext]Cooked meals may provide additional buffs.[/s]
l4941: You''ve obtained an off hand item! Equip it to your off-hand via the Inventory.
l4943:
Just as a tool strikes, [!tnobr][!sHighlight][interact] Gather[/s][/tnobr] again to:
[!sBp]Harvest faster.[/s]
[!sBp]Earn bonus [IconFocus].[/s]
[!sBp]Increase loot drop chances.[/s]
l4944: Sprint off surfaces or ledges to jump and get across small gaps.
l4948: Using gear will diminish its durability. Broken gear is rendered inoperable. Repair gear at a blacksmith or by using repair powder.
l4949: You can cycle a selected item slot.
l4951:
Can be burned in an offering at any [!sHighlight]fire pit[/s]. 

Burning an Effigy inside a [!sPlagued]Pestilence Outbreak[/s] will reveal the heart of the infection. 

Burning an Effigy in a non-infected area will trigger a [!sPlagued]Pestilence Outbreak[/s] in that area, allowing you to find more resources.
l4953: You can swap the selected quick item.
l4955:
Gems can be slotted into gear to infuse it with powerful [!sHighlight]enchantments[/s].
[!sSubtext]Visit an enchanter to infuse gear.[/s]
l4956: Allies healthbars are blue, whilst for Enemies, they’re red.
l4958:
At crafting stations, you can use your [!sHighlight]research knowledge[/s] from [!sHighlight]Scribe Tables[/s] to craft new items, using refined materials.
You can obtain [!sHighlight]new crafting stations[/s] through Maker Danos, either by purchasing them for your home or by upgrading vendor stalls.
l4961: Some surfaces like ladders or vines can be climbed.
l4963: Visit a blacksmith to repair your gear.
l4965: Use tools to [!sHighlight]harvest resources[/s] and [!sHighlight]materials[/s]. Higher tier tools work faster.
l4966: Hold [run] and press against an object to clamber up it.
l4970:
Other Cerim must be [!sHighlight]invited[/s] into your home.

Guests may come and go freely from any house they are invited to. They can also add or remove furniture, and store or collect items from chests within the house.
l4972:
Use a [!sHighlight][action_quickitem][/s] when the situation calls for it.

Quick Items may repair gear, apply temporary buffs, or they may damage and disrupt enemies.
l4974:
Research Papers can be used to unlock [!sHighlight]blueprints[/s] at a [!sHighlight]Scribe Table[/s]. 

Once you have unlocked a blueprint, you can craft the item at a [!sHighlight]crafting station[/s].
l4847:
Learn to exploit enemy attack styles.

Tap [!sHighlight][action_parry][/s] just before an enemy attack lands to deflect the attack.

Successfully parrying will grant [IconFocus] and [!sHighlight]stagger[/s] most enemies.
l4849: The Crucible is an ancient training ground where Cerim can undertake trials and challenges. Every time you enter a trial within the Crucible, you will find a different series of chambers. You must clear all the enemies from each chamber in order to advance to the next one. Dying in the Crucible will return you to the Atrium, and you must begin again from the start. If there are other Cerim still inside the trial, you must wait for them to die or complete it before you can start again.
l5056:
The Pestilence can be [!sHighlight]wielded by the Cerim[/s] to become more powerful.

Future outbreaks will continue to occur over time - but you can also [!sHighlight]trigger an outbreak[/s] by [!sHighlight]burning items[/s] at any fire pit outside of Sacrament.

Burning items will [!sHighlight]increase the plague intensity[/s], and [!sHighlight]add corruptions[/s] to the area. The greater the plague intensity, the greater the rewards. 

Burning Torn Resources will result in fewer corruptions.
l4851: [action_attack] using a mainhand weapon. Tap [action_dodge] to evade enemies. Unleash a [action_charged_attack] to deal extra damage. These actions consume [IconStamina].
l4853:
Runes are powerful special [!sHighlight]abilities[/s].

Most Runes can be performed using [IconFocus].
[!sSubtext]Gain [IconFocus] from attacks or parries.[/s]
l4855: While inside the Crucible, you can collect Traces and Gloamseed from fallen enemies. Traces can be used to invoke effects called Echoes at any point during your run. Echoes bestow various temporary effects upon the player and the world. Gloamseed can be spent elsewhere within the Crucible.
l4857:
While in combat, your [!sHighlight]Combo Meter[/s] keeps track of the number of hits and total damage you deal to enemies within a short period. 

You can regain a small amount of [!sHighlight]health[/s] based on the number of hits dealt when the combo is broken.
l4859:
You have gained a level!
Every level up grants you [!sHighlight]3 attribute points[/s].

To assign your attribute points, open the 
[!tnobr][!sHighlight][start] Menu Selector[/s][/tnobr] and select the [!sHighlight]Stats Menu[/s].
l4861: Sometimes you can use the element of surprise on enemies. Approach enemies from behind to reveal a [IconSkull], then press [attack] to perform a Backstab Attack. [sneak] Sneak to reduce the chance of detection.
l4863: Open the [action_inventory] to equip items and manage collected resources. The [start] Menu Selector also offers quick access to the Inventory.
l4865:
You can combine different [!sHighlight]elemental status effects[/s].

[!sHighlight]Searing[/s] - Dealing Fire damage to an infected target will increase fire damage taken
[!sHighlight]Overload[/s] - Dealing Lightning damage to a burned target will create a powerful lightning AoE
[!sHighlight]Fester[/s] - Dealing Plague damage to a bleeding target will reduce enemy buildup recovery speed
l4867:
When multiple Cerim travel together within a realm, [!sHighlight]more enemies will spawn[/s]; and the health, damage and poise resistance values of enemies will increase.

If you can see another Cerim''s [!sHighlight]portrait[/s], that means they''re part of your group. Cerim who are far away on a realm do not affect local enemy spawning.
l4869: Gear comes in several rarities. Common - Customizable, with 4 gem slots. Rare - Enchantments and 1 gem slot. Plagued - Strong enchantments and 1 gem slot. But carries a negative enchantment. Unique - Special enchantments and a Gem Slot. Uniques cannot be obtained by enchanting.
l4871:
The map reveals your path throughout Isola Sacra.

However, the world is constantly shifting so this path will gradually [!sHighlight]fade[/s] over time. 

Revisit these shrouded regions to discover [!sHighlight]new dangers[/s] and [!sHighlight]loot[/s].
l5058: Increase your Comfort Level to gain various benefits when you sleep in your bed, by placing housing items in your home. Higher quality housing items will give higher levels of comfort. You can get bonus comfort points by placing different items in your home. A higher comfort level will grant increased and additional benefits every time you sleep. [!sHighlight]Bare[/s] (0) - Max [IconHealth] [!sHighlight]Basic[/s] (25) - Max [IconHealth], Max [IconStamina] [!sHighlight]Comfortable[/s] (50) - Max [IconHealth], Max [IconStamina], Max [IconFocus] [!sHighlight]Cozy[/s] (100) - Increased value of benefits [!sHighlight]Luxurious[/s] (150) - Increased value of benefits [!sHighlight]Opulent[/s] (250) - Increased value of benefits
l4873:
Hunt down bounty targets or complete challenges to claim [!sHighlight]daily[/s] and [!sHighlight]weekly[/s] rewards.

Pin activities from the [!sHighlight]Activity[/s] screen and then use the [!sHighlight][action_activities_log][/s] to track progress of accepted activities.

Upon completion, visit Randolph to claim your rewards.
[!sSubtext]Activities can be cancelled at any time.[/s]

[!sHeadingMuted][IconBounty] Bounty Markers[/s]
Active bounties can be located on the map and next to targets.
l4875: Welcome to your new lodgings. You can stash your inventory and share it with other players by using the Community Chest! Every player on the realm can place and remove items from it.
l4877:
Elemental damage builds up status effects.
 
[!sBpIcon][StatFireDamage][/s][!sBpBody][!sFire]Burn[/s] deals damage over time.[/s]
[!sBpIcon][StatColdDamage][/s][!sBpBody][!sIce]Freeze[/s] temporarily solidifies. Physical damage will shatter frozen targets.[/s]
[!sBpIcon][StatLightningDamage][/s][!sBpBody][!sLightning]Shock[/s] will chain to multiple targets.[/s]
[!sBpIcon][StatPlagueDamage][/s][!sBpBody][!sPlague]Infection[/s] temporarily gives the attacker [!sHighlight]Lifesteal[/s].
[!sBpIcon][StatBleed][/s][!sBpBody][!sBleed]Bleed[/s] deals damage to moving enemies.
l4879: Charged Spells must be held to successfully cast. Be careful - These spells can be interrupted while casting.
l4960:
In the [!sHighlight]Compendium[/s] you can find collected knowledge and memories from all Cerim on a realm.

Every tutorial or cinematic scene that has been unlocked by any player can be revisited in the Compendium. Eventually, you will be able to track items discovered and enemies encountered as well.
l4881:
Express yourself by performing [!sHighlight]gestures[/s].

Hold to open the [!sHighlight][action_gesture][/s], then select a gesture and [!sHighlight]release[/s] to perform it.
l4883: With the Mountain Gate breached, the Pestilence is pouring out across Sacra. Pestilence Outbreaks can now emerge anywhere on the island, bringing deadly enemies that may drop special resources. [map] Open the map to find Outbreaks. Return to Sacrament and visit the Rookery to learn more.
l4885:
At [!sHighlight]Scribe Tables[/s], you can learn how to craft items via [!sHighlight]research papers[/s].

Items previously discovered throughout your journey can be chosen to be researched, or you can choose to research a random item within a specific category. Researching an item unlocks the ability to [!sHighlight]craft it[/s] at its respective crafting station.

You can unlock new items at the Scribe Table by upgrading Maker Danos'' Architecture Study.
l4887:
Enemy attacks deal [!tNoBr][IconPoise] [!sHighlight]Poise Damage[/s][/tNoBr].
This can interrupt actions and [!sHighlight]stagger[/s] you.

[!sHeadingMuted]Protection[/s]
Equip heavier gear to raise [StatPoiseDefense] [!tNoBr][!sHighlight]Poise Defense[/s][/tNoBr], such as Mesh or Plate armor.
l4889: Stagger an enemy to briefly lower their guard. Weapons deal [IconPoise] Poise Damage, increasing the white Poise Meter above an enemy. Once full, the enemy is staggered. Alternatively, [parry] Parry just before an enemy attack lands to instantly stagger them. Show the Poise Meter via the Settings Menu
l4891: Within Sacrament, you''ll encounter a variety of vendors, each offering unique services. They might sell special items and resources, enhance or repair gear, or they could even change Sacrament itself! Vendor stalls can be upgraded, further enhancing their services.
l4893:
When you fall in battle you can be [!sHighlight]spoken anew[/s] by your fellow Cerim. 
 
Reviving a companion will cost [!sHighlight]up to 50% of your HP[/s].

Multiple Cerim can also team up to revive one companion.
l5045: Quests can be advanced by any Cerim on a realm. All story progress is shared. Check your quest log in the menu to view quest updates and map markers. If you miss a tutorial or cinematic scene, it can be revisited at any time in the Compendium.
l4895:
You begin with a [!sHighlight]Heal[/s] Utility Rune equipped. This rune allows you to heal by using focus [utility] + [key_special_1].

Utility Runes are character bound abilities which [!sHighlight]aid[/s] and [!sHighlight]assist[/s] in your journey.

[!sHeadingMuted]Equipping[/s]
You can also find other Utility Runes throughout the game, which can be equipped at an Enchanter.
l4897: At Cerim Whispers, you can rest and record your progress. If death should find you, you will incur a durability loss, but be spoken anew at the last Whisper you visited.
l4899: You can now purchase a house in Sacrament. There are several houses in the city to choose from. You can buy or sell a home by interacting with the sign posted outside. Once you own a home, you can buy furniture, crafting equipment, and decor to customize your space.
l4901:
At fire pits, you can [!sHighlight]cook meals[/s] using obtained resources.

Every meal will recover [IconHealth], but some may also provide additional buffs.

Always be on the lookout for food [!sHighlight]recipes[/s] to cook a wider variety of meals.
l4903: You can travel instantly between [!sHighlight]previously visited[/s] Elder Whispers and upgraded Lesser Whispers.
l2273: Increases your resilience to damage, allowing you to withstand more hits before dying.
l2274: Increases the number of attacks, dodges, or blocks you can perform.
l2275: Increases the strength of your character: Weapons that scale with strength receive an attribute bonus.
l2276: Increases the dexterity of your character: Weapons that scale with dexterity receive an attribute bonus.
l2277: Increases your character''s faith: Weapons that scale with faith receive an attribute bonus.
l2278: Increases your character''s intelligence: Weapons that scale with intelligence receive an attribute bonus.
l2279: Raises the limit for equipped gear weight before encumbrance sets in. Your dodge type is determined by movement speed: fast speed allows for a quickstep, normal speed grants a roll, and heavy speed results in a more substantial roll coupled with the capability to execute a shoulder barge, knocking down enemies.
l2280: Increase this attribute to acquire additional focus bars; a new focus bar is granted for every 100 focus points. Focus bars are essential for unleashing powerful rune attacks.
l4975: A collection of knowledge detailing how to fight, defend, and master the art of battle.
l4976: The fundamental rules that shape how the world responds
l4977: An overview of the broader structures, progression features, and world management functions.
